name: example
description: EXAMPLE / starter leakage-safe repo set. The sources below are placeholders (OWNER/...) and are NOT operational — copy this file, replace them with vetted repos, and load that curated config for real scoring. The schema and freeze-window hints are the contract this demonstrates.
strategy: All OBSCURE + OLD. Pin every freeze window `before 2021-01-01` so the maintainer decisions being predicted are HUMAN — a window in the LLM-assisted era makes 'predict what the maintainer did next' circular. Memorization is defended by obscurity (low-traffic, long-lived repos), not by recency: a leakage probe found the scoring model would not recall commit sequences for any repo, famous or obscure, so the retired recent-tier hedge guarded a door that was not open. Set `horizon_days` per repo to its own median release-cycle length (clamped to [14, 90]) so the revealed window means the same thing on every repo — a fixed commit count is ~24 minutes of work on a fast repo and ~46 days on a slow one. Reserve some repos as held_out to score generalization on codebases the agent was never tuned against.

repos.name: obscure-lib-a
repos.notes: Low-traffic library; obscurity is the leakage defense and the pre-2021 bound keeps the ground truth human. horizon_days = this repo's own median release cycle.

repos.name: obscure-lib-b
repos.notes: Rotate freeze points deterministically so repeated runs don't reuse the same answer window.

repos.name: obscure-lib-c
repos.notes: A slow-releasing repo: its median cycle is long, so its window is clamped at the 90d ceiling.

repos.name: heldout-obscure-d
repos.notes: Held out from tuning; used only to measure generalization (see #52). A frequent releaser, so its window sits at the 14d floor.

repos.name: heldout-obscure-e
repos.notes: Held-out obscure repo; the hardest bar — unseen and unlikely to be memorized.
